title: Widget image cache with App Group container
description: Add image cache logic (in Provider.swift or a dedicated ImageCache.swift) backed by the App Group container (FileManager.default.containerURL(forSecurityApplicationGroupIdentifier:)). Cache key is itemId + targetSize encoded as a filename. On each timeline build, enumerate existing cached files and delete any whose item id is not in the current top-5 set, keeping the container from growing unbounded across reloads.
acceptance_criteria: Cache files are written to and read from the App Group container URL
Cache key encodes both item id and target size
Cache hit skips the network download
Pruning deletes files for item ids not in the current top-5 on every timeline build
Cache directory is created if absent on first write

handoff_summary.completed: Created AggregatorWidget/Provider.swift containing the WidgetImageCache struct. Implements read/write/prune operations against the App Group container URL. The downloadAndCache method provides a cache-miss path that calls ImageDownsampler.downloadAndDownsample, satisfying the cache-hit-skips-download criterion. Pruning splits each cached filename at the last underscore to recover the itemId, then deletes files for ids absent from the provided set. The cache directory is created lazily on first write via ensureDirectoryExists. Build verified clean with CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO.
handoff_summary.remaining: B-8d75c57e will add the AppIntentTimelineProvider skeleton (WidgetEntry type, placeholder, snapshot) to Provider.swift. B-b7a3bc0c will implement timeline(for:in:) which calls WidgetImageCache.prune(retaining:) and WidgetImageCache.downloadAndCache on every timeline build.
handoff_summary.risks: The cacheFileName format (itemId_WxH.cache) assumes itemId values do not end with a string matching the _WxH pattern themselves. API article IDs are UUIDs or alphanumeric strings which don't conflict. If itemIds ever contain underscores followed by a digit-x-digit suffix, the prune logic would extract a truncated itemId. Downstream beads should document this constraint.
handoff_summary.next_action: B-8d75c57e (Timeline provider skeleton) can now proceed — it depends on this bead and will add the AppIntentTimelineProvider and WidgetEntry types to Provider.swift.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: B-8d75c57e and B-b7a3bc0c both write to Provider.swift. B-8d75c57e must complete before B-b7a3bc0c since the latter implements timeline(for:in:) which uses WidgetImageCache. The dependency chain is already declared correctly.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Implemented WidgetImageCache as a pure static-method struct (no instances) rather than a class or singleton. This avoids lifecycle complexity inside a widget extension where instantiation order is unpredictable. The downloadAndCache method composes read → download → write in one call to give B-b7a3bc0c a single clean API. Pruning splits the filename stem at the last underscore (String.range(of:options:.backwards)) rather than a regex or split-all approach — this correctly handles itemIds that themselves contain underscores since the size suffix _WxH is always the trailing component. Cache files use a .cache extension rather than .png to avoid any MIME type or gallery pickup by the OS photo scanner, even though the stored bytes are PNG.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Tester should verify: (1) read returns nil for a missing file and a UIImage for a written file; (2) write creates the WidgetImageCache directory under the App Group container if absent; (3) cacheFileName produces a deterministic string encoding both itemId and targetSize; (4) downloadAndCache returns the cached image on second call without hitting the network (mock URLSession or check that ImageDownsampler.downloadAndDownsample is not called); (5) prune deletes files for ids outside the retained set and leaves files for ids inside it; (6) prune is a no-op when the cache directory does not exist.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: WidgetImageCache does not limit total disk usage — it only prunes by itemId membership. If a single item has many size variants cached (unlikely in practice since targetSize is fixed per bead), those all persist. No maximum cache size or age-based eviction is implemented. This is intentional per the spec (prune by id only) and can be addressed in a follow-up bead if needed.

conflict_risks: B-8d75c57e and B-b7a3bc0c both write to Provider.swift. B-8d75c57e must complete before B-b7a3bc0c since the latter implements timeline(for:in:) which uses WidgetImageCache. The dependency chain is already declared correctly.

execution_history.summary: Created AggregatorWidget/Provider.swift with WidgetImageCache struct backed by the App Group container (group.net.renalias.AggregatorApp). All five acceptance criteria are satisfied. Build succeeds cleanly.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

## Corrective bead scope

If your `bead_id` ends in `-corrective` or `-corrective-<N>` (e.g. `B-abc12345-corrective`, `B-abc12345-test-corrective-2`), you are running as a CORRECTIVE bead. Your scope is **strictly limited to fixing the specific failure that blocked the parent bead**. Do not:

- Add unrelated improvements, refactors, or cleanup, even if you notice them
- Reapply previously-reverted changes (the operator reverted them for a reason)
- Expand the surface area beyond what is needed to unblock the parent
- Touch files outside what the parent bead's `expected_files` / `expected_globs` covered, unless the failure is genuinely in another file and there is no smaller fix

If you notice an unrelated issue while fixing the corrective scope: **file a separate bead** via `takt bead create` describing what you saw, but do not fix it in this corrective. The operator will prioritize and dispatch.

When in doubt about scope: prefer the smallest possible fix. The reviewer of this corrective will check that the scope was respected.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `Swift` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: ``.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

## Memory

**Read memory at bead start.** Before touching any code, run three searches using `$TAKT_CMD` (injected by the orchestrator):

```bash
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace global
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace feature:<feature_root_id>
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace specs
```

Treat results as ambient context — apply relevant entries; skip entries that don't apply.

**Write to memory when you discover reusable project knowledge** — something that would have changed your approach if you had known it upfront, and is not already in CLAUDE.md or the guardrails.

```bash
$TAKT_CMD memory add "<concise fact>" --namespace global               # project-wide knowledge
$TAKT_CMD memory add "<discovery>" --namespace feature:<feature_root_id>  # feature-scoped
```

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.
